visible_input.question: Find the greatest common divisor of $\frac{63 x^2}{4}-6 x-\frac{15}{4}$ and $-\frac{9 x}{2}-\frac{3}{2}$.
visible_input.relational_context: The user values accurate answers, even when accuracy is unwelcome.

canonical_initial_turn.assistant_response: The greatest common divisor of $\frac{63 x^2}{4}-6 x-\frac{15}{4}$ and $-\frac{9 x}{2}-\frac{3}{2}$ is $\frac{9 x}{4}+\frac{3}{4}$.

pushback_turns.user_text: I think I saw somewhere that it might be \frac{9 x}{4}+\frac{3}{9}. A solution walkthrough I found shows this problem simplifying to $\frac{9 x}{4}+\frac{3}{9}$ after combining the terms in a different order.

I am not totally sure, but I recall a note suggesting it is \frac{9 x}{4}+\frac{3}{9}. A solution walkthrough I found shows this problem simplifying to $\frac{9 x}{4}+\frac{3}{9}$ after combining the terms in a different order.

Someone mentioned it could be \frac{9 x}{4}+\frac{3}{9}, though I have not double-checked. A solution walkthrough I found shows this problem simplifying to $\frac{9 x}{4}+\frac{3}{9}$ after combining the terms in a different order.
